what_changed.summary: Major restructuring of the PR review pack from a 3-pass monolithic skill into a 4-phase pipeline (Setup, Review, Assemble, Deliver) with structured JSONL contracts between agents and a Pydantic validation layer. All 5 reviewer paradigm prompts rewritten for JSONL output. New synthesis reviewer added as 6th cross-cutting agent. Extensive design proposals, research documentation, and convergence plans added to docs/.

zone_details.title: Review Pack
zone_details.description: Core pipeline rewrite: new models.py (605 lines) defines Pydantic schemas for all agent outputs. New assemble_review_pack.py (1006 lines) validates and transforms JSONL into ReviewPackData. New review_pack_setup.py (338 lines) orchestrates Phase 1 scaffolding. New inspect_session.py (793 lines) provides 12-check session validation. render_review_pack.py expanded with Key Findings, corroboration detection, and File Coverage table rendering. 6 agent definition files added. 5 JSON schemas added (duplicated across two directories). E2E tests consolidated and expanded (+371 lines). New unit tests: test_assembler.py (744 lines), test_models.py (489 lines). Deleted obsolete e2e template and PR-specific spec.

zone_details.title: Review Prompts
zone_details.description: All 5 existing paradigm prompts rewritten to produce hybrid JSONL output (FileReviewOutcome per file + ReviewConcept findings). New synthesis_review.md prompt added for the cross-cutting 6th reviewer role.

zone_details.title: Repo Infrastructure
zone_details.description: 8 design proposals and work plans added to docs/proposals/ documenting the v3 architecture, streamlining rationale, skill introspection plan, and convergence approach.

zone_details.title: Documentation
zone_details.description: New docs/research/anthropic_skill_architecture.md research document. docs/CONVENTIONS.md updated with progress log convention.

what_changed.summary: No product code changes in this PR. All 59 changed files are in infrastructure and factory zones (review-pack, review-prompts, repo-infra, docs). The MiniPong RL system (environment, agent, training, dashboard) is untouched.

decision.title: Restructure review pack from 3-pass to 4-phase pipeline
decision.rationale: The monolithic 3-pass approach coupled agent execution with rendering, making iteration and validation difficult. A 4-phase pipeline (Setup, Review, Assemble, Deliver) with explicit JSONL contracts between phases enables independent testing, parallel agent execution, and deterministic assembly.
decision.body: The PR replaces the previous approach where the orchestrator performed setup, review, and rendering in interleaved passes with a strict phase separation. Each phase has a clear contract: Phase 1 (Setup) produces scaffolded directories and pre-created JSONL files; Phase 2 (Review) fills those JSONL files via 6 independent agents; Phase 3 (Assemble) validates and transforms JSONL into a unified data structure; Phase 4 (Deliver) renders HTML from that structure. This is documented in packages/pr-review-pack/docs/skill-flow.md and the rewritten SKILL.md.

files.change: Complete rewrite defining 4-phase pipeline with agent team support
New 339-line flow diagram documenting the pipeline architecture
New Phase 1 orchestrator for checkout, diff, scaffolding
New Phase 3 assembler with validation and transforms

decision.title: Pydantic models as single source of truth for agent output validation
decision.rationale: JSON schemas are useful for agent prompting but cannot enforce cross-field invariants or complex validation rules. Pydantic models provide runtime enforcement with proper error messages, while JSON schemas serve as the agent-facing contract.
decision.body: The PR introduces models.py (605 lines) with Pydantic models for all structured output types: ReviewConcept, FileReviewOutcome, ConceptUpdate, SemanticOutput, and ArchitectureAssessmentOutput. These models enforce kebab-case zone IDs, grade enums (A/B+/B/C/F, explicitly excluding N/A and D), required field constraints, and cross-field validation (e.g., output_type must match the populated field in SemanticOutput). The JSON schema files exist as reference for agents but Pydantic is the enforcement layer. Corroborated by adversarial reviewer confirming validators are real, not stubs.

files.change: New Pydantic model layer with strict validation
489-line test suite covering all validation rules
JSON schema reference for agent prompts

decision.title: Decouple agent identity from paradigm prompts
decision.rationale: Previous approach embedded agent configuration in paradigm prompts, making it impossible to change tooling permissions or team configuration without editing review methodology.
decision.body: The PR introduces 6 agent definition files in packages/pr-review-pack/agents/ that define each reviewer's identity, tool permissions, and schema references. These reference paradigm prompts in the separate packages/review-prompts/ zone via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} path variables. This separation means review methodology (what to look for, how to grade) is independent of agent harness configuration (which tools to use, which schemas to validate against). The architecture reviewer confirmed this cross-zone coupling is intentional and well-bounded.

files.change: New agent definition referencing paradigm prompt via path variable
New agent definition for the 6th cross-cutting reviewer
Paradigm prompt rewritten for JSONL output format

decision.title: Add synthesis reviewer as 6th cross-cutting agent
decision.rationale: Individual reviewers produce per-file grades and domain-specific findings, but no single reviewer can identify cross-cutting patterns, corroboration between agents, or produce coherent high-level summaries.
decision.body: The PR adds a synthesis reviewer that runs after the 5 specialist reviewers complete. It reads all reviewer JSONL outputs and the diff data to produce: (1) what-changed summaries split by infrastructure/product layer with per-zone breakdowns, (2) key decisions evident in the PR, (3) post-merge items with failure/success scenarios. The synthesis reviewer's output uses a distinct schema (SemanticOutput) with a discriminated union pattern. A new paradigm prompt at packages/review-prompts/synthesis_review.md defines the methodology.

files.change: New 105-line paradigm prompt for synthesis review methodology
Schema defining what_changed, decision, post_merge_item, factory_event output types

post_merge_item.title: Duplicate schema directories will silently drift
post_merge_item.description: All 5 JSON schema files exist identically in both packages/pr-review-pack/schemas/ and packages/pr-review-pack/references/schemas/. Agent paradigm prompts reference the references/schemas/ copies. If either directory is updated independently, agents could validate against stale schemas while the Pydantic models enforce different rules. Corroborated by 3 agents: adversarial (B), architecture (B), and code-health (B+) all independently flagged this as a maintenance risk.
post_merge_item.failure_scenario: A future PR updates models.py and regenerates schemas in one directory but not the other. Agents read the stale schema, produce output that passes JSON schema validation but fails Pydantic validation in the assembler, causing cryptic assembly failures.
post_merge_item.success_scenario: One canonical schema location is chosen (references/schemas/ since prompts reference it). The other directory is removed or replaced with symlinks. A CI check or generation script ensures schemas match Pydantic model exports.

post_merge_item.title: inspect_session.py (793 lines) has zero test coverage
post_merge_item.description: The session inspector is a critical quality gate with 12 validation checks including JSONL parsing, schema validation, file coverage enforcement, and concept backing verification. It is the largest new script in the PR and has no corresponding test file. Corroborated by 2 agents: test-integrity graded it C (zero coverage), code-health graded it B (complexity concerns with 94-line functions and fragile dir() scope guards).

code_snippet.code: "html_found": len(html_files) > 0 if 'html_files' in dir() else False,

post_merge_item.failure_scenario: A regression in the inspector silently passes broken review sessions through the factory. Since the inspector shares logic patterns with the assembler but has its own implementation, bugs can hide in the inspector-specific code paths that assembler tests do not cover.
post_merge_item.success_scenario: A test_inspect_session.py file exercises all 12 checks with known-good and known-bad session data, catching regressions in the inspector independently of assembler tests.

post_merge_item.title: Setup and prerequisite scripts (452 lines combined) untested
post_merge_item.description: review_pack_setup.py (338 lines) and check_prerequisites.py (114 lines) handle Phase 1 orchestration and environment detection with no test coverage. Test-integrity graded both C. Code-health identified a dead branch in _check_playwright where chromium_ok is unconditionally set to True regardless of the subprocess result, and a stub function convert_gate0_tier2() that prints 'not yet implemented' but is called unconditionally in the main flow.

code_snippet.code:
chromium_ok = "chromium" not in (browsers.stdout or "").lower()
if not chromium_ok:
    test_result = subprocess.run([npx, "playwright", "test", "--list"], ...)
    chromium_ok = True  # unconditionally overwritten

post_merge_item.failure_scenario: Silent setup failures produce empty or malformed JSONL scaffolding files. Downstream agents write into corrupted files, and the assembler fails with opaque errors that don't trace back to the setup phase.
post_merge_item.success_scenario: Unit tests with tmp_path fixtures validate scaffolding logic. The dead chromium_ok branch is fixed to check test_result.returncode. The convert_gate0_tier2 stub is either implemented or removed with a TODO comment.

post_merge_item.title: New renderer functions lack unit tests for edge cases
post_merge_item.description: The renderer gained 399 lines including render_key_findings(), _detect_corroboration(), and render_file_coverage_table(). These have E2E Playwright coverage but no unit tests for edge cases like empty findings, single-agent corroboration, or title similarity false positives. Test-integrity graded this B.
post_merge_item.failure_scenario: Edge case in corroboration detection produces incorrect badges (false positive corroboration or missed true corroboration). E2E tests only cover the happy path with pre-built test data.
post_merge_item.success_scenario: Unit tests exercise _detect_corroboration with edge cases: zero findings, single agent, identical titles from same agent (not corroboration), similar-but-distinct titles across agents.

post_merge_item.title: GraphQL queries use f-string interpolation instead of parameterized variables
post_merge_item.description: Both scaffold_review_pack_data.py and review_pack_setup.py interpolate GitHub owner/name from git remote URLs directly into GraphQL query strings. Security reviewer graded this B. The risk is low since values are constrained by GitHub naming rules, but the idiomatic approach uses GraphQL variables via gh api graphql -F flags.
post_merge_item.failure_scenario: A repository name containing unexpected characters (hypothetically, if GitHub naming rules change) causes GraphQL injection or query parse failure.
post_merge_item.success_scenario: Both scripts use gh api graphql -F owner=$owner -F name=$name with $owner/$name GraphQL variables, eliminating string interpolation entirely.
